user_repo.description: A tiny, fast fuzzy file finder for the terminal
user_repo.readme_summary: snipfind is a minimal command-line fuzzy finder that filters file paths and lines as you type. It focuses on speed and a small binary, with interactive selection and ranked results. No indexing, embeddings, or external services.
user_repo.methodology: incremental fuzzy substring matching over filenames with a scoring heuristic for ranking

candidates.description: A command-line fuzzy finder
candidates.readme_summary: fzf is a general-purpose command-line fuzzy finder that lets you interactively filter any list of items. It integrates with shells, editors, and pipelines for fast fuzzy selection.
candidates.methodology: fuzzy substring matching with smart-case scoring over arbitrary input streams

candidates.description: Recursively search directories for a regex pattern, fast
candidates.readme_summary: ripgrep recursively searches the current directory for lines matching a pattern while respecting gitignore rules. It is optimized for speed using parallelism and clever literal optimizations.
candidates.methodology: regex and literal substring search over file contents with gitignore-aware traversal

candidates.description: A simple, fast and user-friendly alternative to find
candidates.readme_summary: fd is a program to find entries in your filesystem with a convenient syntax and sensible defaults. It supports fuzzy-ish substring and regex matching with fast parallel traversal.
candidates.methodology: substring and regex file-path matching with parallel directory walking

candidates.description: A code-searching tool similar to ack, but faster
candidates.readme_summary: The Silver Searcher (ag) searches code quickly while ignoring files specified by .gitignore. It is designed for fast interactive code search from the command line.
candidates.methodology: literal and regex substring search over source trees with ignore-file filtering

candidates.description: Find, filter, preview, pick — fuzzy finder for Neovim
candidates.readme_summary: Telescope is a highly extendable fuzzy finder over lists for Neovim. It provides interactive filtering and preview of files, buffers, and grep results.
candidates.methodology: fuzzy matching over candidate lists with a sorter and interactive picker UI

candidates.description: Fuzzy finder in Rust
candidates.readme_summary: skim is a general fuzzy finder written in Rust that can be used as a library or a standalone tool. It offers interactive filtering over arbitrary streams with ranked results.
candidates.methodology: fuzzy substring scoring over input streams with an interactive selection loop

candidates.description: A fuzzy text selector for files and anything else
candidates.readme_summary: Selecta is a small fuzzy text selector you pipe lists into and interactively narrow down. It is intentionally tiny and unix-pipe friendly.
candidates.methodology: fuzzy substring matching over piped input with a minimal interactive selector

candidates.description: A better fuzzy finder
candidates.readme_summary: fzy is a fast fuzzy text selector for the terminal with an advanced scoring algorithm. It reads a list on stdin and returns the selected item.
candidates.methodology: fuzzy substring matching with a contiguity-aware scoring algorithm for ranking

candidates.description: Simplistic interactive filtering tool
candidates.readme_summary: peco is a simple interactive filtering tool that takes lines on stdin and lets you fuzzily narrow them down. It is commonly used to build interactive command-line workflows.
candidates.methodology: incremental fuzzy filtering over input lines with an interactive selector

candidates.description: Ultra fast grep with interactive query UI
candidates.readme_summary: ugrep is a fast file searcher with an interactive fuzzy query interface and boolean search. It searches file contents and names across large trees quickly.
candidates.methodology: regex and fuzzy substring search over file contents with an interactive TUI

candidates.description: Adds flavor of interactive filtering to the traditional pipe
candidates.readme_summary: percol adds interactive incremental fuzzy filtering to traditional unix pipes. It lets users select lines interactively from any command output.
candidates.methodology: interactive incremental fuzzy filtering over piped command output

candidates.description: Pick file paths out of any text with fuzzy selection
candidates.readme_summary: pathpicker-lite parses file paths out of arbitrary text and lets you fuzzily select among them. It is aimed at quickly opening files referenced in build output or logs.
candidates.methodology: path extraction plus fuzzy substring ranking over detected file references

candidates.description: A smarter cd command with fuzzy directory jumping
candidates.readme_summary: zoxide lets you jump to frequently used directories using fuzzy substring matching and a frecency ranking. It learns your habits and ranks directory candidates accordingly.
candidates.methodology: fuzzy substring matching over a frecency-ranked directory database

candidates.description: Tiny interactive fuzzy picker for snippets and files
candidates.readme_summary: pet-find is a small interactive fuzzy picker that filters snippets and file paths as you type. It is built to be embedded in larger shell workflows.
candidates.methodology: incremental fuzzy filtering over snippet and file lists with interactive selection

candidates.description: Graphs for Everyone — the world's leading graph database
candidates.readme_summary: Neo4j is a native property-graph database for connected data, supporting Cypher queries over nodes and relationships. It models entities and their relations as a first-class graph.
candidates.methodology: property-graph storage and traversal with Cypher query over entity-relationship structures

candidates.description: RDFLib is a Python library for working with RDF
candidates.readme_summary: RDFLib is a pure-Python package for working with RDF triples, including parsers, serializers, and a SPARQL engine. It supports graph stores and ontology-aware querying.
candidates.methodology: RDF triple store with SPARQL querying over subject-predicate-object statements

candidates.description: Apache Jena — a free and open source Java framework for semantic web and linked data
candidates.readme_summary: Apache Jena provides an API and triple store for building semantic-web and linked-data applications. It includes a SPARQL engine, RDF storage, and reasoning over ontologies.
candidates.methodology: RDF triple storage and SPARQL querying with rule-based ontology reasoning

candidates.description: Distributed, fast graph database with GraphQL-like query language
candidates.readme_summary: Dgraph is a horizontally scalable and distributed graph database designed for high throughput. It stores entities and relationships and queries them with a GraphQL-inspired language.
candidates.methodology: distributed property-graph storage with sharded entity-relationship traversal

candidates.description: JanusGraph: an open-source, distributed graph database
candidates.readme_summary: JanusGraph is a scalable graph database optimized for storing and querying graphs with billions of edges. It supports the Gremlin traversal language over entity-relationship data.
candidates.methodology: distributed property-graph storage with Gremlin traversal over entity relations

candidates.description: Blazegraph high-performance graph database supporting RDF/SPARQL
candidates.readme_summary: Blazegraph is a high-performance graph database supporting RDF and SPARQL APIs. It scales to billions of triples and supports ontology-driven reasoning.
candidates.methodology: RDF triple store with SPARQL querying and high-scale ontology indexing

candidates.description: Eclipse RDF4J: scalable RDF for Java
candidates.readme_summary: Eclipse RDF4J is a Java framework for processing and handling RDF data, including triple storage and SPARQL. It provides APIs for ontology-aware semantic applications.
candidates.methodology: RDF triple storage with SPARQL endpoints and ontology inference

candidates.description: An open-source graph database
candidates.readme_summary: Cayley is an open-source graph database inspired by linked-data systems. It stores triples and supports multiple query languages over entity relationships.
candidates.methodology: triple-based graph storage with multi-language traversal over entity relations

candidates.description: ArangoDB — a native multi-model database with graph capabilities
candidates.readme_summary: ArangoDB is a multi-model database supporting graph, document, and key-value data. Its graph engine queries entity-relationship structures with the AQL language.
candidates.methodology: multi-model storage with graph traversal over entity-relationship collections

candidates.description: Examples and ontologies for the Stardog knowledge graph platform
candidates.readme_summary: This repo collects examples, ontologies, and SPARQL queries for the Stardog knowledge graph platform. It demonstrates triple storage and reasoning over enterprise ontologies.
candidates.methodology: RDF triple modeling with SPARQL and OWL ontology reasoning examples

candidates.description: ROBOT is a tool for working with OWL ontologies
candidates.readme_summary: ROBOT is a command-line tool and library for automating ontology development tasks with OWL. It supports converting, reasoning, and validating ontologies and their entity relations.
candidates.methodology: OWL ontology manipulation and reasoning over class and entity relationships

candidates.description: Help documentation for Obsidian, a knowledge base on local Markdown files
candidates.readme_summary: Obsidian is a note-taking app that builds a context graph from backlinks between local Markdown files. This repo hosts its help docs covering linking, graph view, and knowledge organization.
candidates.methodology: bidirectional backlink graph construction over local Markdown notes

candidates.description: A privacy-first, open-source knowledge base
candidates.readme_summary: Logseq is an outliner knowledge base that links blocks and pages into a queryable graph. It works on local Markdown and org-mode files with backlinks and references.
candidates.methodology: block-level backlink graph over local Markdown and org-mode documents

candidates.description: A personal knowledge management and sharing system for VSCode
candidates.readme_summary: Foam is a Roam-inspired personal knowledge management system built on VSCode and Markdown. It creates a graph of notes via wiki-links and backlinks.
candidates.methodology: wiki-link and backlink graph construction over Markdown notes in an editor

candidates.description: A plain text note-taking assistant with zettelkasten features
candidates.readme_summary: zk is a command-line tool for maintaining a Zettelkasten of plain-text Markdown notes. It manages links between notes and lets you query the resulting knowledge graph.
candidates.methodology: zettelkasten link graph over plain-text Markdown notes with query support

candidates.description: The personal knowledge management (PKM) tool that grows as you do
candidates.readme_summary: Dendron is a hierarchical, Markdown-based knowledge management tool built on VSCode. It links and organizes notes into a navigable knowledge base with backlinks.
candidates.methodology: hierarchical note linking and backlink graphs over Markdown documents

candidates.description: Future-proof note-taking and publishing based on Zettelkasten
candidates.readme_summary: Neuron is a command-line app for managing a Zettelkasten of interlinked Markdown notes and publishing them. It generates a navigable web of backlinks between notes.
candidates.methodology: zettelkasten backlink graph generation and static publishing from Markdown

candidates.description: Open-source, collaborative, Roam-like networked thought tool
candidates.readme_summary: Athens is an open-source networked-thought tool inspired by Roam Research. It links blocks and pages into a graph of knowledge with bidirectional references.
candidates.methodology: block-reference graph over documents with bidirectional links

candidates.description: Build your personal knowledge base with Trilium Notes
candidates.readme_summary: Trilium Notes is a hierarchical note application for building large personal knowledge bases. It supports note linking, attributes, and a relationship map between notes.
candidates.methodology: hierarchical note linking with relation maps across a personal knowledge base

candidates.description: A simple and elegant Markdown editor with linking
candidates.readme_summary: MarkText is a Markdown editor that supports note files and inter-document links. It can build a small wiki of linked Markdown notes for a personal knowledge base.
candidates.methodology: Markdown editing with inter-document link tracking for a note wiki

candidates.description: Build a backlink graph from any folder of Markdown notes
candidates.readme_summary: markdown-graph scans a folder of Markdown files and builds a backlink graph you can browse. It is a lightweight tool for visualizing note-linking structure.
candidates.methodology: static backlink graph extraction from Markdown wiki-links

candidates.description: The AI-native open-source embedding database
candidates.readme_summary: Chroma is an open-source embedding database that stores vectors and metadata for semantic search. It is designed to power retrieval-augmented generation applications.
candidates.methodology: approximate nearest-neighbor search over embedding vectors with metadata filtering

candidates.description: High-performance vector similarity search engine
candidates.readme_summary: Qdrant is a vector similarity search engine and database with extended filtering support. It powers semantic search and recommendation over embedding vectors.
candidates.methodology: HNSW approximate nearest-neighbor search over embeddings with payload filtering

candidates.description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database
candidates.readme_summary: Weaviate is an open-source vector database that stores objects and their embeddings for semantic search. It supports hybrid search and modules for various embedding models.
candidates.methodology: approximate nearest-neighbor vector search with optional hybrid keyword scoring

candidates.description: A cloud-native vector database for scalable similarity search
candidates.readme_summary: Milvus is a cloud-native vector database built for scalable similarity search over billions of embeddings. It supports multiple ANN indexes and semantic retrieval workloads.
candidates.methodology: distributed approximate nearest-neighbor search over large embedding collections

candidates.description: A library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors
candidates.readme_summary: FAISS is a library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. It provides a range of ANN indexes used as the core of many vector search systems.
candidates.methodology: indexed approximate nearest-neighbor search over dense embedding vectors

candidates.description: Open-source vector similarity search for Postgres
candidates.readme_summary: pgvector adds vector similarity search to PostgreSQL with new column types and indexes. It enables semantic search and RAG retrieval directly inside a relational database.
candidates.methodology: approximate nearest-neighbor vector indexing as a Postgres extension

candidates.description: Approximate Nearest Neighbors in C++/Python optimized for memory usage
candidates.readme_summary: Annoy is a C++ library with Python bindings for approximate nearest-neighbor search over embeddings. It is optimized for memory use and read-only static indexes.
candidates.methodology: tree-based approximate nearest-neighbor search over embedding vectors

candidates.description: Header-only C++/python library for fast approximate nearest neighbors
candidates.readme_summary: hnswlib is a fast header-only implementation of the HNSW algorithm for approximate nearest-neighbor search. It is widely used as the indexing core for semantic search systems.
candidates.methodology: HNSW graph-based approximate nearest-neighbor search over embeddings

candidates.description: Developer-friendly, serverless vector database for AI applications
candidates.readme_summary: LanceDB is an embedded, developer-friendly vector database for AI applications. It stores embeddings on disk and supports fast semantic search for RAG.
candidates.methodology: on-disk approximate nearest-neighbor search over embeddings with columnar storage

candidates.description: Unified embedding generation and search engine
candidates.readme_summary: Marqo is an end-to-end vector search engine that handles embedding generation and storage. It provides semantic search over text and images for retrieval applications.
candidates.methodology: embedding generation plus approximate nearest-neighbor vector search

candidates.description: A minimal in-process embedding vector store
candidates.readme_summary: tinyvec is a minimal in-process vector store for embedding similarity search. It targets small RAG prototypes that need semantic search without a server.
candidates.methodology: brute-force and ANN nearest-neighbor search over an in-memory embedding store

candidates.description: LLM orchestration framework to build retrieval-augmented applications
candidates.readme_summary: Haystack is an end-to-end framework for building retrieval-augmented and search applications with LLMs. It combines vector search, keyword search, and pipelines into hybrid retrieval systems.
candidates.methodology: pipeline orchestration combining dense vector retrieval with sparse keyword retrieval

candidates.description: Data framework for building LLM applications over external data
candidates.readme_summary: LlamaIndex is a data framework that connects LLMs to external data via indexes, retrievers, and query engines. It combines vector, keyword, and graph retrieval in configurable pipelines.
candidates.methodology: retrieval orchestration combining vector, keyword, and graph indexes into query pipelines

candidates.description: Build context-aware reasoning applications
candidates.readme_summary: LangChain is a framework for building context-aware LLM applications with retrieval and tools. It orchestrates vector stores, document loaders, and chains into hybrid retrieval pipelines.
candidates.methodology: chained retrieval orchestration over vector stores and other retrievers

candidates.description: Reproducible information retrieval with sparse and dense representations
candidates.readme_summary: Pyserini is a toolkit for reproducible information retrieval combining sparse and dense representations. It supports hybrid search that fuses BM25 with dense vector retrieval.
candidates.methodology: hybrid retrieval fusing BM25 sparse search with dense vector search

candidates.description: A Python search engine with hybrid sparse-dense retrieval
candidates.readme_summary: retriv is a Python search engine supporting sparse, dense, and hybrid retrieval out of the box. It fuses keyword and embedding scores into a single ranked result set.
candidates.methodology: hybrid retrieval fusing sparse keyword and dense embedding rankings

candidates.description: The open big data serving engine — store, search, rank and organize
candidates.readme_summary: Vespa is an engine for low-latency computation over large data, combining vector, text, and structured search. It serves hybrid retrieval and ranking pipelines at scale.
candidates.methodology: hybrid serving of vector, keyword, and structured retrieval with learned ranking

candidates.description: Build multimodal AI applications with cloud-native stack
candidates.readme_summary: Jina is a framework for building multimodal AI and neural search applications. It orchestrates embedding, indexing, and retrieval across text, image, and audio modalities.
candidates.methodology: multimodal neural search pipelines combining embeddings and retrieval orchestration

candidates.description: All-in-one embeddings database for semantic search and RAG
candidates.readme_summary: txtai is an all-in-one embeddings database for semantic search, LLM orchestration, and workflows. It combines vector search with keyword and graph components into hybrid pipelines.
candidates.methodology: hybrid embeddings-plus-keyword retrieval with workflow orchestration

candidates.description: Rank fusion and hybrid retrieval evaluation toolkit
candidates.readme_summary: ranx-fusion provides rank-fusion methods for combining multiple retrieval systems into one ranking. It is used to build and evaluate hybrid sparse-dense retrieval pipelines.
candidates.methodology: rank fusion of multiple retrievers to produce a single hybrid ranked list

candidates.description: A hybrid RAG stack combining vectors, keywords and a knowledge graph
candidates.readme_summary: ragstack-hybrid is a reference RAG stack that fuses vector search, BM25, and a knowledge graph for context retrieval. It orchestrates the three retrievers into a single pipeline.
candidates.methodology: multi-retriever orchestration fusing vector, keyword, and graph context sources
